record.field: Bazarr is a companion to Sonarr and Radarr that automatically finds, downloads and keeps your subtitles in sync with your media library.

Bazarr is a companion to Sonarr and Radarr that automatically finds, downloads and keeps your subtitles in sync with your media library.

It manages subtitles per TV show and movie according to your language preferences, querying dozens of subtitle providers and downloading the best match as soon as it becomes available. Bazarr tracks only the series and movies indexed in Sonarr and Radarr (it does not scan disk on its own), and can periodically re-search to upgrade existing subtitles when a better release is found. It targets users running a self-hosted Servarr media automation stack who want hands-off, multilingual subtitle coverage.

Key features
  • Tight integration with Sonarr and Radarr to manage subtitles per series and movie
  • Automatic search and download of missing subtitles in your preferred languages
  • Support for many subtitle providers (OpenSubtitles, Podnapisi and others) with per-provider configuration
  • Release-group and hash matching to improve subtitle-to-video synchronization
  • Scheduled upgrades that replace existing subtitles when a better version appears
  • Subtitle tools: sync, encoding fixes, forced/foreign tracks and download history
Best for

Home media enthusiasts and self-hosters running Sonarr and Radarr who want fully automated, multilingual subtitle management for their Plex, Jellyfin or Emby libraries.

services: bazarr: image: lscr.io/linuxserver/bazarr:1.5.1 restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:6767' environment: - PUID=1000 - PGID=1000 - TZ=UTC volumes: - ./config:/config - {{ movies_path }}:/movies - {{ tv_path }}:/tv container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
